definitions.usage_example: They decided to break up last month.
definitions.alternative_example: They decided to end their relationship.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ellos decidieron terminar el mes pasado.

definitions.usage_example: When does school break up for summer?
definitions.alternative_example: When does school end for summer?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cuándo terminan las clases por el verano?

definitions.usage_example: The party broke up at midnight.
definitions.alternative_example: The party ended at midnight.
definitions.usage_example_es: La fiesta se disolvió a medianoche.

definitions.meaning: Dejar de funcionar (un auto, una máquina)
definitions.usage_example: My car broke down on the highway.
definitions.alternative_example: My car stopped working on the highway.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mi auto se descompuso en la autopista.

definitions.usage_example: He broke down and cried after the news.
definitions.alternative_example: He lost emotional control and cried.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él se derrumbó y lloró después de la noticia.

definitions.usage_example: Can you break down the problem for me?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you explain the problem in parts?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes desglosar el problema para mí?

definitions.usage_example: Someone tried to break in last night.
definitions.alternative_example: Someone tried to enter by force.
definitions.usage_example_es: Alguien intentó entrar a la fuerza anoche.

definitions.usage_example: I need to break in these new shoes.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to wear these new shoes until they are comfortable.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito amansar estos zapatos nuevos.

definitions.usage_example: Two prisoners broke out of jail.
definitions.alternative_example: Two prisoners escaped from jail.
definitions.usage_example_es: Dos prisioneros escaparon de la cárcel.

definitions.usage_example: I always break out when I'm stressed.
definitions.alternative_example: I always get pimples when I'm stressed.
definitions.usage_example_es: Siempre me sale acné cuando estoy estresado.

definitions.usage_example: A fire broke out in the building.
definitions.alternative_example: A fire started suddenly in the building.
definitions.usage_example_es: Estalló un incendio en el edificio.

definitions.meaning: Entrar a la fuerza (a un lugar específico)
definitions.usage_example: They broke into the house and stole the TV.
definitions.alternative_example: They entered the house by force.
definitions.usage_example_es: Entraron a la fuerza a la casa y robaron la TV.

definitions.meaning: Empezar a hacer algo de repente
definitions.usage_example: She broke into a run when she saw the bus.
definitions.alternative_example: She suddenly started running.
definitions.usage_example_es: Echó a correr cuando vio el autobús.

definitions.usage_example: Don't bring up politics at the party.
definitions.alternative_example: Don't mention politics at the party.
definitions.usage_example_es: No saques el tema de la política en la fiesta.

definitions.usage_example: His grandparents brought him up.
definitions.alternative_example: His grandparents raised him.
definitions.usage_example_es: Sus abuelos lo criaron.

definitions.usage_example: You can bring back the book tomorrow.
definitions.alternative_example: You can return the book tomorrow.
definitions.usage_example_es: Puedes devolver el libro mañana.

definitions.usage_example: This song brings back good memories.
definitions.alternative_example: This song makes me remember good times.
definitions.usage_example_es: Esta canción me trae buenos recuerdos.

definitions.usage_example: The stress brought on a headache.
definitions.alternative_example: The stress caused a headache.
definitions.usage_example_es: El estrés me provocó un dolor de cabeza.

definitions.usage_example: You want a challenge? Bring it on!
definitions.alternative_example: You want a challenge? I accept!
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Quieres un reto? ¡Adelante! (¡Venga!)

definitions.usage_example: We need to bring down our costs.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to reduce our costs.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos reducir nuestros costos.

definitions.usage_example: That sad movie really brought me down.
definitions.alternative_example: That sad movie really made me sad.
definitions.usage_example_es: Esa película triste realmente me deprimió.

definitions.usage_example: My new job brings in more money.
definitions.alternative_example: My new job earns more money.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mi nuevo trabajo genera más dinero.

definitions.usage_example: Let's bring in an expert to help us.
definitions.alternative_example: Let's involve an expert to help us.
definitions.usage_example_es: Traigamos a un experto para que nos ayude.

definitions.usage_example: We had to call off the game due to rain.
definitions.alternative_example: We had to cancel the game due to rain.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tuvimos que cancelar el partido debido a la lluvia.

definitions.usage_example: I'm busy now. Can I call you back later?
definitions.alternative_example: I'm busy now. Can I return your call later?
definitions.usage_example_es: Estoy ocupado ahora. ¿Te puedo devolver la llamada luego?

definitions.usage_example: I can always call on my friends for help.
definitions.alternative_example: I can always ask my friends for help.
definitions.usage_example_es: Siempre puedo recurrir a mis amigos por ayuda.

definitions.usage_example: We should call on your grandma this weekend.
definitions.alternative_example: We should visit your grandma this weekend.
definitions.usage_example_es: Deberíamos visitar a tu abuela este fin de semana.

definitions.usage_example: This recipe calls for three eggs.
definitions.alternative_example: This recipe requires three eggs.
definitions.usage_example_es: Esta receta requiere tres huevos.

definitions.usage_example: The protesters are calling for change.
definitions.alternative_example: The protesters are demanding change.
definitions.usage_example_es: Los manifestantes están pidiendo un cambio.

definitions.usage_example: He had to call in sick today.
definitions.alternative_example: He had to phone work to say he is sick.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él tuvo que llamar (al trabajo) para reportarse enfermo hoy.

definitions.usage_example: We need to call in a plumber.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to request a plumber's help.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos llamar a un plomero (fontanero).

definitions.usage_example: I need to sit down and calm down.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to sit down and relax.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito sentarme y calmarme.

definitions.usage_example: I tried to calm him down, but he was too angry.
definitions.alternative_example: I tried to soothe him, but he was too angry.
definitions.usage_example_es: Intenté calmarlo, pero estaba demasiado enojado.

definitions.usage_example: Hey, calm down! It was just an accident.
definitions.alternative_example: Hey, relax! It was just an accident.
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Oye, cálmate! Solo fue un accidente.
definitions.usage_context_es: orden/decirle a alguien que se relaje

definitions.usage_example: Please carry on with your work.
definitions.alternative_example: Please continue with your work.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, continúa con tu trabajo.

definitions.usage_example: He was carrying on about the noise.
definitions.alternative_example: He was complaining loudly about the noise.
definitions.usage_example_es: Estaba haciendo un escándalo por el ruido.

definitions.meaning: Llevar a cabo, realizar (una tarea, un plan)
definitions.usage_example: The team will carry out the plan.
definitions.alternative_example: The team will execute the plan.
definitions.usage_example_es: El equipo llevará a cabo el plan.

definitions.meaning: Transferir (a un nuevo período o lugar)
definitions.usage_example: Will my vacation days carry over to next year?
definitions.alternative_example: Will my vacation days transfer to next year?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Mis días de vacaciones se transferirán al próximo año?

definitions.usage_example: We need to check in at the hotel by 3 PM.
definitions.alternative_example: We need to register at the hotel by 3 PM.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesitamos registrarnos en el hotel antes de las 3 PM.

definitions.usage_example: I'm just calling to check in on you.
definitions.alternative_example: I'm just calling to see how you are.
definitions.usage_example_es: Solo llamo para saber cómo estás.

definitions.usage_example: We have to check out of the room by 11 AM.
definitions.alternative_example: We have to leave and pay for the room by 11 AM.
definitions.usage_example_es: Tenemos que dejar la habitación (hacer check-out) antes de las 11 AM.

definitions.usage_example: Hey, check out that new restaurant!
definitions.alternative_example: Hey, look at that new restaurant!
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Oye, mira ese nuevo restaurante!

definitions.meaning: Sacar prestado (ej. un libro de biblioteca)
definitions.usage_example: I need to check out these books from the library.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to borrow these books from the library.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito sacar prestados estos libros de la biblioteca.

definitions.meaning: Verificar (que alguien o algo esté bien)
definitions.usage_example: I'm going to check on the baby.
definitions.alternative_example: I'm going to see if the baby is okay.
definitions.usage_example_es: Voy a ver cómo está el bebé.

definitions.usage_example: Can you check over my report before I submit it?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you review my report before I submit it?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes revisar mi informe antes de que lo entregue?

definitions.usage_example: I love to check off items on my to-do list.
definitions.alternative_example: I love to mark items as complete on my to-do list.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me encanta marcar tareas (ítems) en mi lista de pendientes.

definitions.usage_example: Come on! We're going to be late.
definitions.alternative_example: Hurry up! We're going to be late.
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Vamos! Vamos a llegar tarde.

definitions.usage_example: Oh, come on! You can't be serious.
definitions.alternative_example: Oh, really! You can't be serious.
definitions.usage_example_es: ¡Oh, venga ya! No puedes hablar en serio.

definitions.usage_example: The show is coming on soon
definitions.alternative_example: The show will start soon.
definitions.usage_example_es: El programa empezará pronto

definitions.usage_example: What time did you come back home?
definitions.alternative_example: What time did you return home?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿A qué hora regresaste a casa?

definitions.meaning: Venir (a la casa de alguien), visitar
definitions.usage_example: Do you want to come over for dinner?
definitions.alternative_example: Do you want to visit my house for dinner?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Quieres venir (a mi casa) a cenar?

definitions.usage_example: Where does this food come from?
definitions.alternative_example: What is the origin of this food?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿De dónde proviene esta comida?

definitions.usage_example: He came up with a great idea.
definitions.alternative_example: He thought of a great idea.
definitions.usage_example_es: Se le ocurrió una gran idea.

definitions.usage_example: Please, come in and sit down.
definitions.alternative_example: Please, enter and sit down.
definitions.usage_example_es: Por favor, entra y siéntate.

definitions.usage_example: When does the new movie come out?
definitions.alternative_example: When is the new movie released?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cuándo se estrena la nueva película?

definitions.usage_example: The sun came out after the rain.
definitions.alternative_example: The sun appeared after the rain.
definitions.usage_example_es: El sol salió después de la lluvia.

definitions.meaning: Dejar (a alguien o algo) en un lugar
definitions.usage_example: Can you drop me off at the next corner?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you leave me at the next corner?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes dejarme en la próxima esquina?

definitions.usage_example: Sales always drop off after Christmas.
definitions.alternative_example: Sales always decrease after Christmas.
definitions.usage_example_es: Las ventas siempre caen después de Navidad.

definitions.usage_example: I dropped off during the boring movie.
definitions.alternative_example: I fell asleep during the boring movie.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me quedé dormido durante la película aburrida.

definitions.usage_example: He dropped out of college after one semester.
definitions.alternative_example: He quit college after one semester.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él abandonó la universidad después de un semestre.

definitions.meaning: Visitar (a alguien) de pasada, sin avisar
definitions.usage_example: I'll drop by your office later today.
definitions.alternative_example: I'll visit your office informally later today.
definitions.usage_example_es: Pasaré por tu oficina más tarde hoy.

definitions.usage_example: Feel free to drop in anytime you're in town.
definitions.alternative_example: Feel free to visit informally anytime you're in town.
definitions.usage_example_es: Siéntete libre de pasar a visitar cuando estés en la ciudad.

definitions.usage_example: I need to figure out how this new software works.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to understand how this new software works.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito entender (descifrar) cómo funciona este nuevo software.

definitions.usage_example: Can you figure out the total cost for me?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you calculate the total cost for me?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes calcular el costo total por mí?

definitions.usage_example: I can't figure him out. He's very quiet.
definitions.alternative_example: I can't understand him. He's very quiet.
definitions.usage_example_es: No puedo entenderlo (descifrarlo). Es muy callado.

definitions.usage_example: I need to find out what time the store closes.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to discover what time the store closes.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito averiguar a qué hora cierra la tienda.

definitions.usage_example: He found out that she was lying.
definitions.alternative_example: He learned that she was lying.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él descubrió que ella estaba mintiendo.

definitions.usage_example: I get up from my bed every morning.
definitions.alternative_example: I rise from my bed every morning.
definitions.usage_example_es: Me levanto de mi cama cada mañana.

definitions.usage_example: He got up quickly when the boss entered the room.
definitions.alternative_example: He rose quickly when the boss entered the room.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él se levantó rápidamente cuando el jefe entró a la habitación.

definitions.usage_example: I get out of the house through the front door.
definitions.alternative_example: I exit the house through the front door.
definitions.usage_example_es: Salgo de la casa por la puerta principal.

definitions.meaning: hacer que una noticia se sepa o difundir
definitions.usage_example: The news about the scandal soon got out.
definitions.alternative_example: The news about the scandal soon became public.
definitions.usage_example_es: La noticia sobre el escándalo pronto se difundió.

definitions.usage_example: The family gets on the train at the station.
definitions.alternative_example: The family boards the train at the station.
definitions.usage_example_es: La familia se sube al tren en la estación.

definitions.usage_example: How are you getting on with your new flatmate?
definitions.alternative_example: Do you have a good relationship with your new flatmate?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cómo te llevas con tu nuevo compañero de piso?

definitions.usage_example: How are you getting on with your homework?
definitions.alternative_example: How are you progressing with your homework?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Cómo estás progresando con tu tarea?

definitions.usage_example: The student gets off the bus at the school stop.
definitions.alternative_example: The student exits the bus at the school stop.
definitions.usage_example_es: El estudiante se baja del autobús en la parada de la escuela.

definitions.usage_example: He got off work early on Friday.
definitions.alternative_example: He finished work early on Friday.
definitions.usage_example_es: Él salió temprano del trabajo el viernes.

definitions.usage_example: The lawyer helped him get off with only a fine.
definitions.alternative_example: The lawyer helped him avoid punishment with only a fine.
definitions.usage_example_es: El abogado le ayudó a librarse con solo una multa.

definitions.usage_example: My friends and I get along really well. We’re always laughing.
definitions.alternative_example: My friends and I have a very good relationship. We’re always laughing.
definitions.usage_example_es: Mis amigos y yo nos llevamos muy bien, siempre nos estamos riendo.

definitions.usage_example: Despite the problems, they are somehow getting along.
definitions.alternative_example: Despite the problems, they are somehow managing.
definitions.usage_example_es: A pesar de los problemas, de alguna manera se las están arreglando.

definitions.usage_example: It took him months to get over his breakup.
definitions.alternative_example: It took him months to recover from his breakup.
definitions.usage_example_es: Le tomó meses superar su ruptura.

definitions.meaning: cruzar o pasar por encima de algo (físico)
definitions.usage_example: I need to get over this high wall.
definitions.alternative_example: I need to climb over this high wall.
definitions.usage_example_es: Necesito pasar por encima de este muro alto.

definitions.usage_example: She needs to get by without money.
definitions.alternative_example: She has to survive without money.
definitions.usage_example_es: Ella necesita arreglárselas sin dinero.

definitions.usage_example: Can you move so I can get by?
definitions.alternative_example: Can you move so I can pass?
definitions.usage_example_es: ¿Puedes moverte para que yo pueda pasar?
